controls.title: Understanding the organization and its context
controls.description: The organization should identify internal and external issues relevant to the purpose of its AI management system.

controls.title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
controls.description: The organization should determine relevant stakeholders and their expectations regarding AI governance.

controls.title: Determining the scope of the AI management system
controls.description: The organization should define and document the scope of the AI management system, including systems and services in scope.

controls.title: Leadership and commitment
controls.description: Top management should demonstrate leadership and commitment to the AI management system.

controls.title: AI policy
controls.description: The organization should establish, approve, and communicate an AI policy aligned with its objectives and obligations.

controls.title: Organizational roles, responsibilities and authorities
controls.description: Roles and responsibilities for AI governance should be assigned and communicated.

controls.title: Actions to address risks and opportunities
controls.description: The organization should identify AI-related risks and opportunities and define actions to address them.

controls.title: AI objectives and planning to achieve them
controls.description: The organization should define measurable AI governance objectives and plans to achieve them.

controls.title: Competence
controls.description: Personnel involved in AI activities should be competent through education, training, or experience.

controls.title: Communication
controls.description: Relevant internal and external communications related to AI governance should be defined and managed.

controls.title: Documented information
controls.description: The organization should maintain documented information necessary for the AI management system and its operation.

controls.title: Operational planning and control
controls.description: The organization should plan, implement, and control AI lifecycle processes.

controls.title: AI risk assessment
controls.description: AI risk assessments should be performed before deployment and when significant changes occur.

controls.title: AI system lifecycle management
controls.description: Formal lifecycle controls should exist for design, development, testing, deployment, monitoring, and decommissioning.

controls.title: AI system impact assessment and technical controls
controls.description: The organization should implement appropriate technical and organizational controls including testing, oversight, robustness, and supplier governance.

controls.title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
controls.description: KPIs and evaluation criteria should be defined to monitor the performance and effectiveness of the AI management system.

controls.title: Internal audit
controls.description: The organization should conduct internal audits at planned intervals.

controls.title: Management review
controls.description: Top management should review the AI management system at planned intervals.

controls.title: Continual improvement
controls.description: The organization should continually improve the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the AI management system.

controls.title: Nonconformity and corrective action
controls.description: The organization should manage AI governance nonconformities, root causes, and corrective actions.
